title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable Object
description: API object representing a custom theme configuration table associated with a top table.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: deleteMe
methods.description: Deletes this custom theme table from the configuration.

methods.name: getCell
methods.description: Returns the cell at the specified row and column.

methods.name: moveColumns
methods.description: Moves the specified columns from one table to another.

properties.name: columns
properties.description: Returns the columns in this table.

properties.name: id
properties.description: Returns the unique ID of this table.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: name
properties.description: Gets and sets the name of the table as seen in the user interface.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentTableColumn
properties.description: Returns the column in the top table that references this custom theme table.

properties.name: rows
properties.description: Returns the rows (configurations) defined for this table and provides the functionality to create new rows.

classType.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

deleteMe.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.deleteMe Method
deleteMe.description: Deletes this custom theme table from the configuration.
deleteMe.return_description: Returns true if the delete was successful.

parameters.name: deleteColumns
parameters.description: If true, this deletes the columns in the custom theme table. If false, it moves them back to the top table.

getCell.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.getCell Method
getCell.description: Returns the cell at the specified row and column.

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: The index of the column the cell is in. An index of 0 is the first column and does not include the name column.

parameters.name: row
parameters.description: The index of the row the cell is in. An index of 0 is the first row and does not include the header row.

moveColumns.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.moveColumns Method
moveColumns.description: Moves the specified columns from one table to another.
moveColumns.return_description: Returns an array of the columns created due to the move.

parameters.name: columns
parameters.description: An array of the columns within this table that you want to move.

parameters.name: targetTable
parameters.description: The table you want to move the columns to. The target must be either a top table or a custom theme table.

columns.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.columns Property
columns.description: Returns the columns in this table.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationCustomThemeTable_var.columns

id.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.id Property
id.description: Returns the unique ID of this table.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = configurationCustomThemeTable_var.id

name.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.name Property
name.description: Gets and sets the name of the table as seen in the user interface.

syntax.python:

propertyValue = configurationCustomThemeTable_var.name

# Set the value of the property.
configurationCustomThemeTable_var.name = propertyValue

objectType.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

propertyValue = configurationCustomThemeTable_var.objectType

parentTableColumn.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.parentTableColumn Property
parentTableColumn.description: Returns the column in the top table that references this custom theme table.

rows.title: ConfigurationCustomThemeTable.rows Property
rows.description: Returns the rows (configurations) defined for this table and provides the functionality to create new rows.

propertyValue = configurationCustomThemeTable_var.rows
